nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coral reefs, weed and rubble areas, and seagrass beds typically at 2–15 m depth

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on amphipods, polychaetes, and molluscs, plus algae and benthic invertebrates. Widely sold for its tendency to pick at Aiptasia and Majano nuisance anemones in reef tanks. Generally accepts a variety of prepared and frozen marine foods in captivity — mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, finely chopped krill/squid/shrimp/clam, quality marine pellets, and frozen marine-algae preparations — though some specimens are initially shy feeders and may need whole or live food to be coaxed onto prepared diets. Several small meals per day are generally advised.

goodWith: small to medium peaceful reef fish

summary: A small Indo-Pacific filefish popular for its taste for Aiptasia and Majano nuisance anemones. Generally hardy and peaceful, but commonly considered reef-safe only with caution — individuals have been known to nip soft corals, zoanthids, and feather dusters.
careNotes:
The Aiptasia Eating Filefish (also widely sold as Bristletail or Matted Filefish) is a small, somewhat cryptic Monacanthid from shallow Indo-West Pacific reefs, weed beds, and rubble. It is most often purchased not as a display fish in its own right but as a biological control for Aiptasia and Majano anemone outbreaks in reef tanks. Most specimens do develop a taste for these pest anemones, though there are reports of individuals that ignore Aiptasia entirely, so a backup chemical or manual removal plan is generally advised.

Reef compatibility is the species' most important caveat. While it is marketed as reef-safe, virtually every careful source classifies it as reef-safe with caution — once Aiptasia is exhausted, many individuals have been known to begin sampling soft corals, zoanthids, polyps, feather dusters, and small ornamental shrimp. Aquarists with prized colonies of softies or zoas should monitor closely and be prepared to remove the fish. SPS-dominated tanks have been reported to fare better than mixed or softie-heavy systems.

Generally considered peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but commonly territorial toward its own species and other filefish, so a single specimen per tank is the standard recommendation unless the system is very large. Provide plenty of rockwork with crevices to retreat into, plus open swimming space — the species is an active, curious swimmer that has been reported to spend much of its time exploring the rocks for food. A fairly varied diet is generally advised: in addition to anemones, offer mysis, brine, finely chopped seafood, and marine-algae preparations a few times daily.

Cross-source notes: adult size — LiveAquaria states ~5.5 in (~14 cm), FishBase reports 14.0 cm SL max, BlueZoo/AlgaeBarn cite ~10 cm — written 10–14 cm range to span them. Temperature — LiveAquaria 72–78°F (22–26°C), FishBase wild range 22–28°C, Bulk Reef Supply 75–80°F (24–27°C); averaged to 23–27°C. Minimum tank — sources converge on 30 US gal (~114 L) but the species is an active swimmer that benefits from extra footprint and is more reliably distracted from coral-nipping in a larger, established system, so biased up to 150 L (~40 US gal) following the playbook's larger-tank rule. Aiptasia-eating efficacy varies markedly by individual; the species-level behavior is genuine but not guaranteed.

Trade-name variant: also sold as 'Clown Filefish' — another common retail name for Acreichthys tomentosus, used interchangeably with Aiptasia Eating, Bristletail, and Matted Filefish. No distinct color morph; same species and husbandry apply.
breedingNotes: Oviparous in the wild. Captive breeding is uncommonly reported and is not considered an established hobby project; the species is overwhelmingly traded as wild-caught specimens.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection landing page used to confirm common-name aliases (Aiptasia Eating / Bristletail / Matted Filefish), scientific name Acreichthys tomentosus, and the general LiveAquaria filefish husbandry guidance (adequate hiding plus ample swimming space). The species-specific LiveAquaria product page was found via search at /product/2562 and used for the detailed husbandry figures: 30 US gal minimum, 5.5 in max, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level Moderate, peaceful temperament except toward conspecifics, omnivore diet, reef-safe with caution due to coral nipping.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-confirmed scientific name Acreichthys tomentosus (Linnaeus, 1758), family Monacanthidae, order Tetraodontiformes, max length 14.0 cm SL, depth 2–15 m, marine reef-associated/non-migratory, Indo-West Pacific distribution (Sri Lanka to New Caledonia, north to southern Japan), wild temperature range 22–28°C (modelled preferred 25.4–29.1°C), solitary behavior, diet of amphipods/polychaetes/molluscs, oviparous reproduction, IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply
additional.notes: Used as additional secondary because the Reef2Reef wiki page for this species returned HTTP 403. Cross-checked 30 US gal minimum, 75–80°F (23–27°C), pH 7.7–8.5, SG 1.020–1.026, omnivore behavior with supplemental feeding required, caveat that not all individuals consume Aiptasia, peaceful curious temperament, and the reef-safety variability (may nip soft corals, feather dusters, small crustaceans).

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous in the wild. No established captive-breeding protocol; the species is essentially always wild-caught in the trade.
